cards.back: A theoretical limit that describes the maximum theoretical speedup that can be achieved in a parallel system, which is determined by the fraction of the program that cannot be parallelized
The ability of a parallel system to maintain its performance as the number of processors increases, which is limited by the communication overhead and the load balancing of tasks
The ratio of the time taken by a parallel program to execute to the time taken by a serial version of the same program, often expressed as a factor or a multiple
The process of measuring the execution time of a parallel program on a specific hardware configuration, which is used to evaluate the performance of the program and the system
The rate at which a Graphics Processing Unit (GPU) can perform calculations or execute instructions, which is often measured in terms of floating-point operations per second (FLOPS) or megaflops
